title: Minimum Difference Between Highest and Lowest of K Scores

testcases.input: nums = [90], k = 1 Output: 0 Explanation: There is one way to pick score(s) of one student: - [90]. The difference between the highest and lowest score is 90 - 90 = 0. The minimum possible difference is 0.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: There is one way to pick score(s) of one student: - [90]. The difference between the highest and lowest score is 90 - 90 = 0. The minimum possible difference is 0.

testcases.input: nums = [9,4,1,7], k = 2 Output: 2 Explanation: There are six ways to pick score(s) of two students: - [9,4,1,7]. The difference between the highest and lowest score is 9 - 4 = 5. - [9,4,1,7]. The difference between the highest and lowest score is 9 - 1 = 8. - [9,4,1,7]. The difference between the highest and lowest score is 9 - 7 = 2. - [9,4,1,7]. The difference between the highest and lowest score is 4 - 1 = 3. - [9,4,1,7]. The difference between the highest and lowest score is 7 - 4 = 3. - [9,4,1,7]. The difference between the highest and lowest score is 7 - 1 = 6. The minimum possible difference is 2.
